author: nero_eth
content:
Since we're experimenting with splitting the 2nd half of ACDT into breakout rooms, I wanted to propose a tentative agenda for the EIP-7928 side of the call:

* BAL optimization status (focus on I/O benchmarking)
* [eth/71](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-8159.md) implementation status 
* [snap/2](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-8189.md) intro + proposal from <@465903001156976682> to **not reuse* message IDs from snap/1 but instead define new ones
  * see: https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11391#discussion_r2939292215

It'd also add discussions around EIP-8037 (esp. focusing on the gas accounting topic that <@583892532644151312> brought up in the most recent ACDE, cc <@968526635735326741> ), if time and demand (although, we'd have the repricing breakout call on wednesday for that too - so probably less critical to discuss today(?))

cc <@&1460290804898861117>

author: matkt0
content: <@792404665068158998> if we can make a decision about the <@465903001156976682> question async

author: nero_eth
content:
**Let me quickly summarize the BAL breakout:**

* Optimization benchmarking are on-track
  * next steps: benchmark batch IO on shadowfork + bloatnet using worst-case stateful benchmark tests
* Based on the benchmarking results
  * Do we keep the state locations for batch IO improvements?
  * Consider reordering BAL ordering (moving from lexicographical ordering to access based ordering)?
* [eth/71](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-8159.md) - not discussed: client pls have a look
* [snap/2](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-8189.md) - quickly introduced: relevant for Geth, Besu, Nethermind and Reth (likely not relevant for Erigon's sync)

### Open question regarding snap/2:
**Should we reuse the Message IDs from snap v1 for snap v2, or should we keep counting?**

another question I'd add:
**Should we make sure that snap v2 is rolled out across clients with Glamsterdam to avoid some clients not supporting it (thus not being able to support nodes that already use it during sync)?** 
Would be great to get a temp check if client think this is feasible for glamsterdam. The efficiency improvements and simplification are quite significant, thus we should actively consider it.

author: nero_eth
content: <@&1460290804898861117> - Feel free to chime in with opinons 🙂

author: yperbasis
content: <@&1416139337179074590> we have an environment variable to disable BAL optimizations in EVM execution, `IGNORE_BAL` (see https://github.com/erigontech/erigon/pull/19903). Plz update https://notes.ethereum.org/@ethpandaops/bal-devnet-2 (and 3) - I don't have permissions to edit it. cc <@845538039169286164>

author: qu0b
content: thanks will update, & do you have the prefetch optimization?

author: .mholt
content: I'm still working on the prefetch code, it will likely hit in bal-devnet-3 as it will be tricky to cherry pick into bal-devnet-2.  Note that the IGNORE_BAL I'm currently doing local fixes on as I discovered in performance testing that we weren't getting the level of parallelization I was expecting this is a bug which will hit later today or tomorrow.

author: .mholt
content: As a little more context around prefetch we are partially doing this via read ahead caching, but I discovered in testing that we have a cross thread DB look-up which is pausing our parallel executers, which also needs to be removed before we see the benefits of this.
